questions.question: What is the topic for Week 3?
options: A. THE DEITY OF JESUS PART 1
D. THE WAY, THE TRUTH, AND THE LIFE
questions.explanation: The specific topic given for Lesson Three is THE DEITY OF JESUS (PART 2).

questions.question: Which of the following was the topic for Week 2?
options: A. THE DEITY OF JESUS PART 1
C. THE WAY, THE TRUTH, AND THE LIFE
questions.explanation: The Deity of Jesus Part 1 was the topic for Week 2.

questions.question: But if I do, though you do not believe Me, believe the works, that you may know and believe that the ________ is in Me, and I in Him.
questions.explanation: The memory verse (John 10:38) says '...believe that the Father is in Me, and I in Him.'

questions.question: In which book, chapter, and verse is the memory verse for this week found? (Use format: Book Chapter:Verse)
questions.explanation: The memory verse is explicitly cited as John 10:38.

questions.question: Type out the memory verse for Week 3 in full (NKJV).
questions.answer: But if I do, though you do not believe Me, believe the works, that you may know and believe that the Father is in Me, and I in Him.
questions.explanation: Full text recall for John 10:38.

questions.question: What does Jesus tell His listeners to believe, even if they do not initially believe Him, so that they may know the Father is in Him?
questions.explanation: He says 'believe the works, that you may know and believe that the Father is in Me'.

questions.question: Where is this verse found? 'Jesus said to him, “I am the way, the truth, and the life. No one comes to the Father except through Me.'
questions.verse_text: Jesus said to him, “I am the way, the truth, and the life. No one comes to the Father except through Me.
questions.explanation: This is verse 6 of the main Bible Passage, John 14:6-11.

questions.question: In the Bible Passage (John 14:8), which disciple said to Jesus, 'Lord, show us the Father, and it is sufficient for us'?
questions.explanation: The text reads 'Philip said to Him, “Lord, show us the Father...' (John 14:8).

questions.question: In the Bible Passage (John 14:10), Jesus stated that the words He speaks, He speaks entirely on His own authority.
questions.explanation: He said 'The words that I speak to you I do not speak on My own authority...' (John 14:10).

questions.question: The introduction states that it is easy for all people to accept the fact that Jesus is God because the evidence is undeniable.
questions.explanation: The introduction says 'While it may be difficult for some people to believe or accept the fact that Jesus is God, the evidence cannot be denied.'

questions.question: Which Bible reference is used in the introduction to state that Jesus was in the form of God and equal with God?
questions.explanation: The introduction cites '(Philippians 2:6)'.

questions.question: What is the heading of the first outline in this lesson?
questions.explanation: Outline I is titled 'THE MIRACLES HE PERFORMED'.

questions.question: According to the definition provided in Outline I, what does a miracle involve?
options: B. The direct and powerful action of God transcending ordinary laws of nature
C. A magic trick performed to deceive the masses
D. A natural event with good timing
questions.explanation: The text states it 'involves the direct and powerful action of God, transcending the ordinary laws of nature'.

questions.question: Which Old Testament references are used to demonstrate that miracles show God's involvement in human history?
questions.explanation: The text cites '(Exodus 14:21-23; Joshua 10:12-14)' regarding demonstrating His involvement in human history.

questions.question: According to Outline I, why were many miracles performed by Jesus?
options: B. To prove that He is God
questions.explanation: The lesson states: 'Many miracles were performed by Jesus as proof that He is God.'

questions.question: Which of the following references is cited for Jesus walking on water?
questions.explanation: The text cites '(Matthew 14:25)' for walking on water.

questions.question: Which miracle is associated with the reference John 2:7-9 in the lesson?
options: D. Raising people from the dead
questions.explanation: The lesson lists 'turned water to wine (John 2:7-9)'.

questions.question: The references John 11:43-44 and Luke 7:13-15 are used in the lesson to support the fact that Jesus healed the sick.
questions.explanation: Those verses are used to support that He 'raised people from the dead', not healed the sick.

questions.question: The lesson claims that no other religion can claim the resurrection of its founders.
questions.explanation: Outline I explicitly states: 'No other religion can claim the resurrection of its founders.'

questions.question: What is the heading of the second outline in this lesson?
questions.answer: THE WAY, THE TRUTH, AND THE LIFE
questions.explanation: Outline II is titled 'THE WAY, THE TRUTH, AND THE LIFE'.

questions.question: What part of speech did Jesus use to distinguish Himself as 'the Way, the Truth, and the Life'?
questions.explanation: The lesson states: 'Jesus used the definite article to distinguish Himself...'

questions.question: According to Outline II, a 'way' is defined as what?
questions.explanation: The text defines: 'A way is a route leading somewhere.'

questions.question: Which verses are cited to prove that there is no other path to Heaven except Jesus?
questions.explanation: The text states 'There is no other path to Heaven except Jesus (John 14:1-3; Acts 4:12).'

questions.question: Jesus, as the incarnate Word of God (John 1:1), is described as the source of all truth in which reference?
questions.explanation: The lesson says 'is the source of all truth (John 8:32).'

questions.question: According to Psalm 119:142, truth represents the ________ of God.
questions.explanation: The text says 'truth represents the law of God'.

questions.question: During which event did Jesus remind His followers of the basic points of the law and give new commands, equating Himself with God?
options: B. The Sermon on the Mount
questions.explanation: The text says 'during the sermon on the mount, He reminded them of the basic points...'

questions.question: According to Matthew 5:17, Jesus said He came to destroy the demands of the law and the prophets.
questions.explanation: The lesson states 'Jesus said He came to fulfil the demands of the law and the prophets'.

questions.question: Jesus spoke of His authority over life being the source of life in which of the following pairs of verses?
questions.explanation: The outline cites '(John 10:10b; 14:19)' directly after 'Jesus spoke of His authority over life being the source of life'.

questions.question: Jesus laid down His life for His '________', and then took it back again (John 10:17-18).
questions.explanation: The lesson states: 'He laid down His life for His ‘sheep,’ and then took it back again'.

questions.question: Which verses are cited to show that Jesus brought true deliverance from a life of bondage to sin and death to a life of victory?
questions.explanation: The text lists '(John 3:15-16; 10:28)' for this point.

questions.question: The conclusion of the lesson states that there is still much controversy over whether Jesus Christ is God.
questions.explanation: The conclusion states: 'There is no controversy, Jesus Christ is God.'

questions.question: Where is this verse found? '“If you had known Me, you would have known My Father also; and from now on you know Him and have seen Him.”'
questions.verse_text: “If you had known Me, you would have known My Father also; and from now on you know Him and have seen Him.”
questions.explanation: This is verse 7 from the main Bible Passage, John 14:6-11.

questions.question: Which of these is NOT one of the miracles of Jesus listed in the lesson?
questions.explanation: Parting the Red Sea (Exodus 14) is cited as a general miracle of God, but not listed under 'miracles were performed by Jesus'.

questions.question: By reminding His followers of the basic points of the law and giving new commands, Jesus was equating Himself with God as the authoritative standard of righteousness.
questions.explanation: The lesson explicitly says 'thereby equating Himself with God as the authoritative standard of righteousness.'

questions.question: Where is this verse found? 'Believe Me that I am in the Father and the Father in Me, or else believe Me for the sake of the works themselves.'
questions.verse_text: Believe Me that I am in the Father and the Father in Me, or else believe Me for the sake of the works themselves.
questions.explanation: This is verse 11 from the main Bible Passage.
